summary: Excellent matrix recording captures a solid 7/6/87 show with standout Franklin’s Tower and Shakedown Street performances.
review: The newly‑added HQ front‑of‑house/matrix source delivers crystal‑clear audio that far outshines earlier releases of this tour. Musically the concert is strong – a relaxed first set highlighted by Franklin’s Tower, Big River and Desolation Row, followed by an energetic second set featuring Shakedown Street, Women Are Smarter and a sprawling Knocking on Heaven’s Door – though a few moments (Samson, Don’t Ease Me In) feel a bit distracted. Overall the show is enjoyable and the recording makes it highly listenable.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review shows an excellent recording but a moderately good performance with some uneven spots, leading to a blended rating near the upper‑mid range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: HQ front‑of‑house/matrix mix praised as clear, balanced and a big improvement over previous audience recordings; no major flaws noted.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is chill and expansive, building nicely to Franklin’s Tower; second set opens powerfully with Shakedown Street and maintains momentum despite a few distracted moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Noted for his big smile onstage; no specific guitar critique.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard work highlighted as driving the tonal development of the Shakedown Street jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums and Space are longer and more intricate than typical 1987 performances, receiving positive mention.
